title: Delete SQLite/LanceDB and make Postgres+VectorChord the only database
description: Why this exists: the project is greenfield and should not keep separate local SQLite/LanceDB and hosted Postgres storage paths. What needs to be done: remove SQLite/LanceDB config, code, CLI/docs/tests, promote Postgres+VectorChord as the single database/search store for all modes, and delete obsolete compatibility wrappers and abstractions.
acceptance_criteria: SQLite and LanceDB code/config/dependencies/docs/tests are removed; all runtime query/ingest/API/CLI paths use Postgres+VectorChord; no local/hosted storage branch remains; quality gates pass.
close_reason: Completed SQLite/LanceDB deletion and Postgres+VectorChord cutover. Removed legacy stores/wrappers/tests, routed API/CLI/MCP/helpers through Postgres search/resource stores, regenerated contract JSON, refreshed architecture docs, and verified with ruff, compileall, full pytest, web typecheck, Worker typecheck, and Worker TS tests.

title: Harden hosted broker correctness paths
description: Implement the hosted broker hardening plan: fix usage gating, lease safety, provider replay, reservation lifecycle, billing idempotency, hosted auth/session hardening, and verification for real hosted indexing/search.
design: Preserve Yutome's ProseMirror-plugin style: keep core contracts small and compose hosted providers, gates, adapters, and search modes through explicit state/effects.
acceptance_criteria: Paid/external hosted operations are metered before execution; leases prevent split-brain writes; provider success is durable without replay; reservations reconcile or release; billing idempotency handles same-unit grants; hosted auth/session invariants are covered by tests; targeted and full quality gates pass.
notes: Implemented Postgres bind-parameter hardening after real smoke exposed psycopg AmbiguousParameter failures. Added explicit casts for nullable text/timestamptz, text arrays, and COALESCE/ANY/ALL paths across hosted jobs, indexing, billing, entitlements, runtime, and search-store SQL. Added hosted SQL guardrail test plus optional live psycopg/Postgres validation via YUTOME_TEST_POSTGRES_DSN. Verification: uv lock --locked; hosted pytest 251 passed, 1 skipped; full pytest 516 passed, 2 skipped; Docker Postgres live validation 7 passed; Railway deploys for api, worker, cron-maintenance, cron-source-refresh, and cron-billing-export all SUCCESS. Real Railway smoke indexed four videos (OEDoJyhQhXs, lwH29W1M57A, 3kqQ9MzKhYQ, KeaaYdD8Lsw), wrote 28 chunks and 28 embeddings, lexical/semantic/hybrid hosted searches returned rows, and ledger summary showed 56 reservations all reconciled with no reserved_units stuck.
close_reason: Completed hardening implementation and verification: tests green, Railway services deployed, real 4-video hosted indexing/search smoke passed, ledger reservations reconciled.

title: Implement hosted account creation and session bootstrap
description: Why: hosted mode currently has Postgres identity tables and Cloudflare account-grant handling, but no production self-service account creation or account-session issuer. What: add a composable account control-plane path that creates verified users/workspaces, provisions starter entitlements/provider allocations, issues signed account sessions for hosted MCP consent, and records decisions/tests. Keep provider credentials and billing adapters outside the account core per the Yutome ProseMirror plugin philosophy.
notes: Implemented Cloudflare hosted account-session cookie resolver slice: yutome_account_session HttpOnly cookie is now the production transport for the signed v1 account-session token; x-yutome-account-session remains as a dev/test fallback only when the cookie is absent. Validation remains in the existing verifier path (HMAC, audience, expiry, workspace selection, normalized workspace ids). Tests added/updated for cookie success, missing session, invalid cookie, workspace mismatch, header fallback, and cookie-over-header precedence. Verification: cd cloudflare/yutome-capsule && npm run test:ts; npm run typecheck.
close_reason: Implemented hosted account bootstrap core/schema slice with targeted tests

title: Hosted: execute source discovery jobs
description: Why this exists: source-refresh-tick currently enqueues discover_source jobs for non-video sources, but the hosted worker only claims index_video, leaving production discovery stuck. What needs to be done: add a real discovery executor for channel/handle/playlist/subscription sources, dispatch discover_source from the worker, persist cursors/status, enqueue idempotent index_video jobs, and verify with targeted tests plus a real hosted smoke path.
notes: Live Railway verification after 761dd6e: deployed API/worker/cron services successfully. Fresh workspace ws_railway_real_20260526182549 indexed YouTube video OEDoJyhQhXs via real hosted worker with Webshare/Gemini/Voyage provider calls, wrote transcript tx_5e29b4312046a5e73af6fabb, 2 chunks, 2 embeddings. VectorChord BM25 search-smoke for 'mushrooms' returned 2 rows. Direct DB check confirmed chunks join to workspace-owned profile sip_7c77109d1b55a081b8c6b0a3. Live HTTPS /tools/call find returned 2 rows with citation https://youtube.com/watch?v=OEDoJyhQhXs&t=0s. Decisions: profile ids must be workspace-scoped; search-store allocation may be unpinned or match the workspace profile. Verification: uv run pytest -q => 499 passed, 1 skipped; Railway real-indexing-smoke/search-smoke/API find passed.

title: Production follow-up: replace hosted pairing code with account workspace selection
description: Hosted Cloudflare OAuth still uses a static YUTOME_PAIRING_CODE and configured workspace/install/account ids. Replace hosted pairing-code consent with account-backed session/workspace selection so hosted OAuth grants represent the real Yutome user/workspace.
acceptance_criteria: Hosted OAuth consent does not require YUTOME_PAIRING_CODE; user identity and workspace are resolved from an account session or equivalent hosted auth source; grants are created for the selected workspace/user; tests cover missing session, workspace mismatch, and successful account-backed grant creation; connector-only pairing remains unchanged.
notes: Integrated worker branch result locally: hosted OAuth pairing now uses signed x-yutome-account-session with HMAC verification, audience/expiry checks, workspace-selection validation, and grant validation for user/workspace/client/scope/audience. Connector-only pairing remains on YUTOME_PAIRING_CODE only. Verification: npm run test:ts => 23 passed; npm run typecheck passed; full uv run pytest -q => 467 passed, 1 skipped.
close_reason: Completed: hosted OAuth grants are account-session/workspace scoped instead of static hosted pairing code; connector-only behavior preserved.

title: Review fix: redact hosted provider failure metadata
description: A review found provider exception text can be persisted to usage ledger metadata, including proxy credentials or provider key fragments. Redact sensitive tokens before hosted provider failure metadata is appended.
acceptance_criteria: Provider failure UsageEvent metadata never contains URL userinfo, bearer/API-key-looking values, proxy passwords, or obvious provider tokens from exception messages; focused provider-wrapper tests pass.
notes: Decisions relied on: provider failure text can persist to the usage ledger and must be redacted before classification/metadata, not just hidden from MCP responses. Options weighed: provider-specific redaction versus a generic hosted failure sanitizer; chose a generic sanitizer in hosted errors to cover Webshare proxy userinfo, API-key/token assignments, bearer strings, and common provider token shapes without coupling to provider clients. Files touched: src/yutome/hosted/errors.py, tests/test_hosted_provider_wrappers.py. Verification: uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_provider_wrappers.py -q passed 6/6. Follow-up risks: generic regex redaction cannot prove every future provider credential shape; provider-specific wrappers should continue avoiding raw secrets in exception messages where possible.
close_reason: Completed provider failure metadata redaction with focused provider-wrapper tests passing.

title: Production follow-up: wire real hosted entitlements into UsageGate
description: The live hosted MCP adapter currently has durable UsageGate/ledger plumbing, but default_usage_context/default_voyage_usage_context are permissive stubs when no real entitlement provider is injected. Build/read workspace entitlements and balances from Postgres so hosted query/provider calls deny by default when limits/balance are absent.
acceptance_criteria: Runtime hosted API uses a Postgres-backed UsageContextProvider for search_store and Voyage query operations; missing workspace entitlement/balance denies before provider/search calls; tests cover deny-by-default, soft/hard deny policy, and balance exhaustion; no permissive unlimited defaults in production app builder.
notes: Completed entitlement-backed hosted UsageGate wiring. Decisions: production build_postgres_app now injects PostgresUsageContextProvider for search_store and voyage; unconfigured HostedMcpQueryAdapter defaults deny closed instead of granting unlimited units; missing policy/allocation hard-denies, missing/exhausted balance soft-denies so hybrid can fall back to lexical while semantic fails loudly; billing hard_limits feed hard denial and soft_limits feed soft denial; exact int/Decimal unit handling from billing slice is preserved. Files touched: src/yutome/hosted/entitlements.py, http_api.py, mcp_query.py, gate.py, models.py, billing.py, provider_wrappers.py, cli_helpers.py, repositories.py, allocation_policy.py, tests/test_hosted_entitlements.py, tests/test_hosted_mcp_query.py, tests/test_hosted_http_api.py, tests/test_hosted_repositories.py, tests/test_hosted_billing.py, tests/test_hosted_usage.py. Verification: uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_entitlements.py tests/test_hosted_mcp_query.py tests/test_hosted_http_api.py tests/test_hosted_billing.py tests/test_hosted_usage.py tests/test_hosted_repositories.py -q => 95 passed; uv run pytest -q => 467 passed, 1 skipped; npm run test:ts => 23 passed; npm run typecheck passed; real Voyage embed smoke returned one 1024d vector; real Gemini smoke returned ok; Webshare-backed transcript fetch for OEDoJyhQhXs returned 137 English generated segments; Railway API healthz/readyz live checks passed. Risks: production API deployment must be refreshed from this branch; mcp.yutome.com remains blocked by Cloudflare zone access issue yt-indexer-af9.
close_reason: Completed: production hosted MCP query path uses Postgres-backed entitlements/balances and fail-closed defaults with tested soft/hard denial semantics.

title: Review fix: hosted find citations and fallback semantics
description: A review found live hosted find emits youtube_url from internal video surrogate ids instead of youtube_video_id, and semantic queries silently fall back to lexical on provider/vector availability issues. Fix citation generation and make fallback behavior match the hosted retrieval contract.
acceptance_criteria: find rows prefer youtube_video_id for youtube_url while preserving internal video_id; tests use distinct internal/youtube ids; semantic provider/vector availability errors fail loudly while hybrid can fall back to lexical with explicit notes/events; targeted hosted MCP query tests pass.
notes: Decisions relied on: hosted MCP adapter owns contract-row shaping and fallback policy at the hosted boundary; SearchStore rows may carry distinct internal video_id and public youtube_video_id; provider/vector availability fallback is allowed only for hybrid because hybrid has a lexical leg, while semantic must fail loudly. Options weighed: keeping availability fallback shared by semantic/hybrid was rejected because it silently changes semantic semantics; moving URL selection into SearchStore was rejected to avoid hidden coupling outside the adapter boundary. Files touched: src/yutome/hosted/mcp_query.py; tests/test_hosted_mcp_query.py. Verification: uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_mcp_query.py tests/test_hosted_search_store.py -q => 48 passed in 0.87s. Follow-up risks: semantic vector-store availability errors currently re-raise the original store exception after recording failure, matching existing non-fallback behavior; no follow-up issue filed.
close_reason: Completed hosted MCP find citation and fallback semantics fix; targeted tests pass.

title: Review fix: fail closed hosted API and sanitize readiness
description: A review found hosted HTTP API auth fails open when YUTOME_HOSTED_API_TOKEN is unset, while /readyz can expose raw DB exception strings. Fix the live API to fail closed for tool/resource traffic by default and sanitize readiness errors without leaking connection/user/host secrets.
acceptance_criteria: Tool/resource endpoints reject requests when hosted API token is missing or invalid; health/readiness semantics remain usable for ops; /readyz exception bodies do not include raw DB URLs or credentials; focused hosted HTTP/runtime tests pass.
notes: Decisions relied on: hosted tools/resources must fail closed under the default HTTP auth dependency; missing YUTOME_HOSTED_API_TOKEN is treated as service misconfiguration (503 api_token_unconfigured), while missing/invalid bearer auth remains 401; health/readiness stay unauthenticated; explicit auth_dependency or expected_api_token keeps tests ergonomic without hidden global env coupling. Options weighed: considered preserving fail-open for local tests, rejected because it was the production vulnerability; considered returning raw readiness exception strings with redaction, rejected in favor of stable readiness error codes plus sanitizing returned check payloads. Files touched: src/yutome/hosted/http_api.py, src/yutome/hosted/runtime.py, tests/test_hosted_http_api.py, tests/test_hosted_runtime_cli.py. Verification: uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_http_api.py tests/test_hosted_runtime_cli.py -q => 33 passed in 2.67s. Follow-up risks: readiness check payload sanitization intentionally preserves short snake_case error codes only; any future rich diagnostics should be structured into non-secret fields instead of raw exception messages.
close_reason: Completed fail-closed hosted API auth and sanitized readiness error leakage; focused tests pass (33 passed).

title: Review fix: hosted Cloudflare tenant identity must not trust fallback headers
description: Claude review found Cloudflare relay routing accepts workspace/install from request headers and falls back to local/desktop under the hosted path. Split local bridge fallback from hosted mode and ensure tenant identity comes from verified OAuth/bridge credentials only.
acceptance_criteria: Cloudflare tests prove hosted mode rejects missing verified tenant identity, never trusts raw tenant headers for hosted routing, no idFromName(default), and local/BYO connector compatibility is explicit.
notes: Fixed review blocker in Cloudflare tenant resolution. Hosted mode now requires verified OAuth props for MCP routing, uses configured bridge identity for relay routing, ignores raw x-yutome-workspace-id/x-yutome-install-id headers in hosted mode, and fails before Durable Object routing when hosted tenant identity is missing. connector_only mode keeps explicit local/desktop compatibility. Verification: npm run test:ts passed 10 tests; npm run typecheck passed; rg 'idFromName("default")' cloudflare/yutome-capsule/src/*.ts returned no matches.
close_reason: Completed review blocker: hosted mode rejects missing verified tenant identity, ignores raw tenant headers for relay routing, requires OAuth props for MCP routing, and keeps connector_only local defaults explicit.

title: Review fix: hosted active transcript and search substrate contract
description: Claude review found active transcript replacement likely conflicts with partial unique index and schema does not follow plan's videos.active_transcript_version_id direction. Also SearchStore is labeled VectorChord while lexical SQL uses Postgres FTS fallback. Correct schema/query helpers and docs/tests.
acceptance_criteria: Schema and SearchStore tests use videos.active_transcript_version_id for active state; replacement SQL is safe; backend/fallback naming is explicit; tests cover VectorChord default vs Postgres FTS fallback contract.
notes:
Decisions: active transcript state now uses videos.active_transcript_version_id; transcript_versions no longer carries is_active in the hosted schema; removed the partial unique active-transcript index. Replacement SQL upserts the transcript version and updates the video pointer. Search plans join videos and only read chunks whose transcript_version_id matches the active pointer. VectorChord remains the default backend/extension check; lexical SQL is explicitly labeled postgres_fts_fallback until real vchord_bm25 SQL is implemented, with fallback metadata on lexical/hybrid usage. Options rejected: did not fake vchord_bm25 SQL; did not move indexing execution order in this ownership slice. Files touched: src/yutome/hosted/migrations.py, src/yutome/hosted/search_store.py, tests/test_hosted_postgres.py, tests/test_hosted_search_store.py, plus tests/test_hosted_indexing_smoke.py assertion updated to the new SQL contract. Tests: uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_postgres.py tests/test_hosted_search_store.py passed 13/13. Courtesy run uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_indexing_smoke.py -q now has one remaining failure in test_usage_reservation_hook_receives_operation_ids_and_stable_keys, where reservations are denied instead of reserved; not caused by this SQL contract change. Follow-up risk for Poincare indexing work: current indexing plan schedules search_store.replace_active_transcript before chunk/chunk_embedding upserts, but the plan requires building the replacement fully before swapping videos.active_transcript_version_id; indexing execution/order should adapt in its slice.
Follow-up issues created: yt-indexer-58r for Poincare/indexing active-pointer swap ordering; yt-indexer-33f for the remaining hosted indexing smoke usage-reservation denial.
close_reason: Completed hosted active transcript pointer and search substrate naming contract; targeted postgres/search_store tests pass. Force-closed despite open Phase 4 dependency because this review-fix slice is complete and follow-up issues yt-indexer-58r and yt-indexer-33f are filed.

title: Review fix: fail-closed UsageGate policy and balances
description: Claude review found UsageGate can allow calls when policy.allowed_operations and balance units are empty. Fix hosted enforcement to fail closed unless an explicit policy/balance grants the operation/units or an explicit unlimited sentinel is used.
design: Decision: UsageGate now fails closed. Empty EntitlementPolicy.allowed_operations no longer grants all operations; use allowed_operations for explicit operation grants or allow_all_operations=True for an explicit all-operation policy. WorkspaceBalance now denies any estimated unit not present in remaining_units unless that unit is listed in explicit unlimited_units. Missing balance units and over-limit balances both return insufficient_balance before provider calls.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove missing policy operation and missing balance unit deny before provider calls; existing explicit allowances remain green; denial messages are user-facing.
notes: Files changed: src/yutome/hosted/models.py; tests/test_hosted_usage.py; tests/test_hosted_provider_wrappers.py; tests/test_hosted_allocation_policy.py; tests/test_hosted_allocations.py; tests/test_hosted_gemini_integration.py; tests/test_hosted_embedding_integration.py; tests/test_hosted_webshare_integration.py; tests/test_hosted_indexing_smoke.py. Tests added/updated cover missing policy operation denial, missing balance unit denial, explicit unlimited unit allowance, and provider denied-before-call. Verification: uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_usage.py tests/test_hosted_allocations.py tests/test_hosted_allocation_policy.py tests/test_hosted_provider_wrappers.py tests/test_hosted_gemini_integration.py tests/test_hosted_embedding_integration.py tests/test_hosted_webshare_integration.py => 43 passed; uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_*.py => 101 passed. Ruff check attempted with uv run ruff check on touched files but ruff is not installed in this environment. Follow-up risk: code/tests are in an already dirty untracked hosted tree, so commit/stage boundaries need coordination with concurrent hosted broker work.
close_reason: Implemented fail-closed UsageGate policy and balance semantics with explicit operation grants/unlimited units and hosted test coverage.

title: CI: run ruff+pytest+contract on push/PR and gate the PyPI release
description: Only .github workflow is release.yml (PyPI on v* tags) running uv build + twine check with NO test/lint gate, so a regressing/broken build can publish. Default pytest skips all DB tests (green-on-skip). Add GitHub Actions: ruff + compileall + pytest + doctor contract on every push/PR, with a live-Postgres+VectorChord service-container job (tensorchord/vchord-suite) so DB tests execute; gate release.yml on it. DISJOINT from in-flight code workstreams — buildable in parallel now.

title: Hosted: rate limiting + audit logging on HTTP/MCP and self-hosted remote
description: No rate limiting anywhere (grep: only upstream-provider 429 handling). Only abuse backstop is usage quota depletion. docs/remote-access.md:198-203 lists per-IP/per-token rate limiting + audit logging as required before public hosted remote MCP. Add request rate limiting (per token/workspace/IP) + authn/authz audit logging.
notes: Spec ready (workflow wf_9c5532a2-229). DESIGN: RateGate (request-frequency) complementing UsageGate (prepaid-units: 'deny when rate > tier requests_per_minute' vs 'deny when balance < estimate'). LAYERED: (L1 edge) CF WAF IP rule + Workers Rate Limiting binding in yutome-mcp-agent.ts keyed on workspace_id+grant_id from props, cheap abuse floor on /mcp before any origin/psycopg cost; (L2 origin, authoritative) a RateGate dependency on the 3 auth deps (default/account/cli) keyed on resolved workspace_id, run BEFORE UsageGate.reserve, the only layer seeing dashboard/CLI/billing + the plan. STORE: in-process token bucket on app.state now (single uvicorn process, off the pool); defer shared store (Postgres counter or workspace-keyed DO) to multi-replica. KEY: per-workspace (+ per-IP edge floor; per-tool index bucket as v2). PLAN: new requests_per_minute on EntitlementPolicy (+ requests_per_minute_jsonb column, baseline edit). RESPONSE: hard 429 + Retry-After + IETF RateLimit-* headers; edge maps to retryable MCP error. EXEMPT: /healthz,/readyz,signature-verified Stripe webhook; tighter coarse IP limit on /account/login/start + OAuth/pairing/signup. OPEN (asked user): plan-driven vs flat cap; single-process vs multi-replica (gates in-process store).
close_reason: Origin RateGate (token-bucket, pre-usage-gate) with 429+Retry-After+RateLimit-* headers, health/stripe-webhook exemptions, audit logging, EntitlementPolicy.requests_per_minute. Adversarial review found + fix-up resolved 3 must-fix holes: proxy-aware right-most-XFF client IP, key on token/IP not unvalidated workspace header, LRU-bounded buckets. Full suite green. Committed ab5e5b8.

title: Hosted: server-side account-session revocation
description: verify_account_session_token (account.py:250-324) validates HMAC+exp only and never reads account_sessions.status/revoked_at; account_auth_dependency (http_api.py:949-967) just loads the workspace. A leaked 1h session token cannot be killed. Add status/revoked_at consult in the verify path + an UPDATE...SET status='revoked' write path + a CLI/endpoint to revoke. CLI tokens already do this (http_api.py:1005) — mirror it.
close_reason: Server-side account-session revocation: account_auth_dependency rejects revoked sessions (by session_hash); POST /account/session/revoke revokes caller's own session; load/revoke_account_session_sql builders. Passed adversarial review (clean) + full suite. Committed ab5e5b8.

title: Hosted: add Postgres RLS (row-level security) for tenant isolation
description: Audit top blocker #1. schema.py has zero ROW LEVEL SECURITY; isolation rests on ~30 hand-written workspace_id predicates with no DB backstop (one miss cross-leaks). Add ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + per-table workspace_id policies, set via SET LOCAL app.workspace_id / current_setting on a per-request connection. SEQUENCES AFTER the psycopg pool (yt-indexer-e6g) since it rides per-request connection checkout. Until then add a test asserting every search_store/indexing/resources query carries a workspace_id predicate.
notes: Blocked-by: yt-indexer-e6g (pool). Spec after Stripe (yt-indexer-zcw) lands so it reflects post-rewrite schema.py.

title: Replace Polar with Stripe metered billing (Checkout + Billing Meters + Customer Portal)
description: Polar prohibits YouTube-adjacent SaaS. Swap to Stripe usage-based billing. Checkout mode=subscription (omit quantity for metered price) + Customer Portal are ~1 day. Billing Meters add ~half a day: create Meter (POST /v1/billing/meters), metered Price bound via recurring.meter, report usage via POST /v1/billing/meter_events using our deterministic dedupe key as the meter_event identifier (Stripe dedupes >=24h) — this finally closes the dead usage->export loop. New tables stripe_customers / stripe_meter_exports / stripe_webhook_events replace the polar_* tables (greenfield, rewrite baseline). Env: STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, price/meter IDs. Webhook signature verify replaces Polar's.
notes:
Spec produced (workflow wf_21b0b538-292). OPEN DECISIONS: (a) single composite 'credits' meter vs 4 unit meters; (b) keep prepaid credits path or pure-metered; (c) free-tier $0 subscription at signup vs lazy at checkout; (d) official stripe SDK vs urllib parity. Recommend: 1 credits meter, pure metered, keep WorkspaceBalance for free-tier quota.
Workstream B implemented: replaced Polar with Stripe metered (single composite 'credits' meter, pure metered/arrears).

KEY DECISION / DEVIATION FROM SPEC edit_plan: spec edit_plan proposed 4 Stripe meters (media_seconds/total_tokens/queries/vectors); the user's locked product decision overrides this with ONE composite 'credits' meter (event_name yutome.credits). Implemented credits_from_billable_units() collapsing the 4 billable units via STRIPE_CREDIT_UNIT_WEIGHTS into one credits value; all other units stay internal quota/cost-visibility. So one export row per usage event (not per-unit fan-out), dedupe key stripe:{ws}:{usage_event}:{credits}.

Files changed (src): hosted/billing.py (full rewrite: StripeCustomer/StripeMeterExportEvent/StripeWebhookEvent models, verify_stripe_webhook_signature, process_stripe_webhook_event, stripe_meter_event_payload, upsert_stripe_*_sql + claim/finish raw locking SQL, load_stripe_customer_sql, dropped all Polar/credit-ledger symbols + dropped credit_entries input from derive_workspace_balance_snapshot + balance_reconciliation_input_sql now usage+reservation only); hosted/schema.py (stripe_customers/stripe_meter_exports/stripe_webhook_events tables, dropped billing_customers/billing_exports/credit_ledger_entries/polar_webhook_snapshots); hosted/ledger.py (close-the-loop: append() enqueues stripe_meter_exports in-txn when status in {succeeded,released} AND active stripe_customers row, via _ACTIVE_STRIPE_CUSTOMER_SQL); hosted/runtime.py (worker billing_export_once->stripe_meter_export_once, _post_polar_usage_export->_post_stripe_meter_event urllib form-POST to /v1/billing/meter_events w/ Idempotency-Key, timestamp-out-of-range->skipped terminal); hosted/http_api.py (POST /billing/checkout + /billing/portal behind account_auth_dependency w/ lazy customer creation, POST /webhooks/stripe public signed; STRIPE_* env vars; _stripe_post urllib helper); hosted/alembic baseline downgrade list; cli/actions.py + cli/hosted.py (hosted run stripe-meter-export).

Tables: stripe_customers (1/workspace, workspace_id UNIQUE + stripe_customer_id UNIQUE), stripe_meter_exports (UNIQUE source_event_dedupe_key=PK id=meter identifier; closes dead usage->export loop), stripe_webhook_events (PK=Stripe evt_ id, exactly-once via on_conflict_do_nothing). Dropped polar_webhook_snapshots entirely; greenfield baseline rewritten in place.

Stripe call sites: billing.py:stripe_meter_event_payload (meter-event mapping); runtime.py:_post_stripe_meter_event (worker POST); http_api.py:_stripe_post + billing_checkout/billing_portal (Customer/Checkout/Portal); webhook verify at billing.py:verify_stripe_webhook_signature (ts.body HMAC-SHA256, raw whsec_ key).

Tests rewritten: test_hosted_billing.py (Stripe shapes incl. signature accept/tamper/expired/missing, exactly-once webhook PK, credits collapse), test_hosted_postgres.py (live: 3 upserts + claim/finish locks + re-enqueue no-op + webhook PK dedupe), test_hosted_http_api.py (/webhooks/stripe signed + /billing/checkout+portal), test_hosted_runtime_cli.py (stripe_meter_export worker + reconcile w/o credits).

LIVE-PG BUG FOUND+FIXED: claim_stripe_meter_exports_sql had IndeterminateDatatype on jsonb_build_object('lease_owner', %(lease_owner)s) and updated_at=%(now)s — added ::text and ::timestamptz casts (verified against disposable postgres:16-alpine). Also fixed a pre-existing unrelated B905 ruff error in tests/test_hosted_search_store.py (zip strict=) to keep the gate green; not a workstream-A file.

OPEN ITEMS: (1) docs/architecture/diagrams/hosted-er-entitlements-billing.svg + hosted-er-usage-metering.svg still show old Polar tables/columns — they are hand-positioned rendered SVGs (no Mermaid render pipeline here); partial text swaps would leave stale columns/relationships, so they need full regeneration from the updated Mermaid in hosted.md. The cluster-map SVG WAS updated (pure table-name list). (2) credits unit weights in STRIPE_CREDIT_UNIT_WEIGHTS are placeholder values — confirm commercial rates before launch.

Gates: uv run ruff check src/ tests/ = clean; uv run pytest -q = 365 passed, 1 skipped (live PG ran via OrbStack).
Implementation COMPLETE in working tree (agent was accidentally stopped before reporting, but work is coherent): Polar fully removed (0 src refs); new tables stripe_customers/stripe_meter_exports/stripe_webhook_events; single composite credits meter (STRIPE_CREDIT_UNIT_WEIGHTS); /webhooks/stripe + /billing/checkout + /billing/portal routes; meter-export worker stripe_meter_export_once wired in runtime.py; usage->meter loop closed via ledger _enqueue_stripe_meter_exports (only for active subscribers, in-tx idempotent insert); prepaid credit_ledger_entries path dropped (0 refs); urllib (no SDK). Gates GREEN: ruff clean, 365 passed/1 skipped. Spot-check clean: webhook HMAC verify (ts.body, raw whsec_, compare_digest, 300s tolerance, multi-sig) correct; reserve/settle double-spend intact. title: Phase 5 MCP OAuth docs cross-check
description: Read-only current-docs cross-check for the hosted Cloudflare MCP OAuth/revocation implementation. Use official MCP and Cloudflare docs to verify token props, protected resource metadata, audience/resource binding, revocation expectations, and Worker OAuthProvider shape.
acceptance_criteria: Beads notes cite official docs checked and list concrete implementation constraints/recommendations for yt-indexer-pvq.6.13; no source files edited.
notes:
2026-05-26 current-docs cross-check for hosted MCP OAuth/revocation. Read-only repo work; source files were not edited. Official docs checked via Browser/curl:
- MCP Authorization latest 2025-11-25: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/authorization
- MCP Streamable HTTP transport latest 2025-11-25: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/transports
- Cloudflare Remote MCP server guide: https://developers.cloudflare.com/agents/guides/remote-mcp-server/
- Cloudflare Securing MCP servers guide: https://developers.cloudflare.com/agents/guides/securing-mcp-server/
- Cloudflare McpAgent API: https://developers.cloudflare.com/agents/api-reference/mcp-agent-api/
- Cloudflare workers-oauth-provider README, current package metadata observed as v0.7.0 / latest release May 21 2026 on GitHub: https://github.com/cloudflare/workers-oauth-provider and raw README https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cloudflare/workers-oauth-provider/main/README.md

MUST-HAVE protocol/platform constraints for yt-indexer-pvq.6.13:
1. Hosted HTTP MCP auth is transport-level OAuth. If the Worker supports auth on Streamable HTTP, it SHOULD conform to the MCP auth spec; stdio rules are irrelevant for hosted /mcp.
2. The protected MCP server is the OAuth resource server. It MUST expose OAuth Protected Resource Metadata with authorization_servers. Current spec supports either a WWW-Authenticate resource_metadata URL on 401 or well-known metadata; clients must prefer the header when present and otherwise probe path-specific /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp before root /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource.
3. 401 challenges SHOULD include scope guidance. For Yutome's initial read-only hosted endpoint, use scope="yutome.search.read" on the Bearer challenge and expose matching minimal scopes in protected-resource / authorization metadata where supported.
4. The authorization server metadata must be discoverable. Latest MCP says AS must provide OAuth Authorization Server Metadata or OIDC discovery, and clients must support both. workers-oauth-provider implements /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server when configured with authorizeEndpoint/tokenEndpoint/clientRegistrationEndpoint.
5. MCP clients MUST send resource in both authorization and token requests. Use canonical resource https://mcp.yutome.com/mcp, including the path, because the endpoint path identifies the protected MCP resource. The Worker/resource server must validate presented tokens were issued for this resource/audience and reject tokens for other resources.
6. Access tokens must be sent in Authorization: Bearer headers on every HTTP request, even within the same logical MCP session. Never accept access tokens in query strings.
7. Invalid/expired token => 401. Insufficient scope => 403, preferably with WWW-Authenticate Bearer error="insufficient_scope", resource_metadata, and a scope parameter naming the required scopes.
8. Token passthrough is forbidden. The MCP client's Yutome token must never be accepted as a provider token or forwarded to Gemini, Voyage, Google/YouTube, Webshare, Postgres, Railway, etc. If upstream provider calls are needed, Yutome acts with its own server-side credentials/allocation path.
9. OAuth code flow needs PKCE. Current MCP latest strengthens this: clients must verify PKCE support in AS metadata; if code_challenge_methods_supported is absent clients must refuse. Configure workers-oauth-provider with allowPlainPKCE: false so metadata advertises S256-only behavior.
10. Refresh tokens, if issued to public clients, must rotate. workers-oauth-provider has refreshTokenTTL default 30 days, 0 disables refresh tokens, undefined makes them non-expiring. Its documented compromise allows two valid refresh tokens per grant at a time to survive transient client failure; do not assume strict single-valid-refresh-token semantics in app audit/reuse logic.
11. Streamable HTTP /mcp is one endpoint supporting POST and GET. POST requests must include Accept: application/json and text/event-stream; JSON-RPC notifications/responses accepted by server should return 202. Requests return application/json or text/event-stream. GET may open SSE or return 405.
12. Streamable HTTP sessions are separate from OAuth grants/tokens. If the server emits MCP-Session-Id during initialize, clients must include it on later requests; missing session can be 400, terminated session should be 404, and DELETE with MCP-Session-Id may terminate. Session IDs are not authorization and must not replace per-request Bearer validation.
13. Streamable HTTP servers must validate Origin on incoming connections to mitigate DNS rebinding; invalid Origin should be 403.
14. Current MCP 2025-11-25 adds Client ID Metadata Documents as SHOULD and treats Dynamic Client Registration as MAY / fallback/backward compatibility. This is the main delta from docs/hosted-provider-broker-plan.md, which lists POST /register as a hosted endpoint. Do not override the plan, but implementers should not rely exclusively on DCR for future MCP clients; enabling workers-oauth-provider CIMD support may be needed later and requires Cloudflare's global_fetch_strictly_public compatibility flag.

Yutome product choices that remain plan-owned, not protocol-mandated:
1. user_id, workspace_id, grant_id, client_id, scopes, audience, token_version in token props are Yutome's workspace-isolation model. MCP requires valid audience/resource/scopes, but not these exact prop names.
2. Initial scope yutome.search.read is a product scope. Protocol only requires consistent challenge/metadata/scope enforcement.
3. Revoking an OAuth grant without touching YouTube source grants/provider credentials is Yutome product policy. It aligns with the plan and token-passthrough rules, but MCP does not define Yutome's grant tables.
4. Lexical search not requiring Voyage and semantic/hybrid reserving Voyage query embedding are Yutome UsageGate/product choices, not MCP auth requirements.
5. Cloudflare Worker as edge and Railway/Postgres as query/ingest backends remain plan architecture choices.

Cloudflare Worker/library recommendations for yt-indexer-pvq.6.13:
1. Keep OAuthProvider wrapping the /mcp API handler. Cloudflare docs show either apiRoute: "/mcp", apiHandler: MyMCP.serve("/mcp") or apiHandlers: { "/mcp": MyMCP.serve("/mcp") }. McpAgent.serve handles Streamable HTTP automatically.
2. Configure OAuthProvider metadata explicitly: authorizeEndpoint "/authorize", tokenEndpoint "/token", clientRegistrationEndpoint "/register" if keeping DCR for compatibility, scopesSupported ["yutome.search.read"], allowPlainPKCE false, accessTokenTTL short, refreshTokenTTL deliberately chosen or 0 if avoiding refresh tokens in the first slice.
3. Configure resourceMetadata explicitly rather than relying on request-origin defaults: resource "https://mcp.yutome.com/mcp", authorization_servers containing the Yutome AS origin, scopes_supported ["yutome.search.read"], bearer_methods_supported ["header"], resource_name such as "Yutome MCP".
4. In completeAuthorization props include only Yutome routing/authz facts needed by the API handler: user_id, workspace_id, grant_id, client_id, scopes, audience/resource, token_version/expires if the app needs them. Do not put provider credentials, YouTube OAuth tokens, Webshare credentials, Gemini/Voyage keys, Railway/Postgres credentials, or raw upstream tokens in props.
5. Cloudflare docs say props are available on ctx.props / McpAgent props and can contain identity/tokens for external calls, but for Yutome the product constraint is stricter: no upstream provider tokens in MCP props; use server-side account/workspace allocation lookups instead.
6. workers-oauth-provider stores OAuth records in KV binding OAUTH_KV. It hashes secrets and encrypts props with token-derived material; userId and metadata are intentionally not encrypted because they support audit/revocation enumeration. Avoid putting sensitive provider material in userId/metadata; use opaque grant/user IDs.
7. Implement revocation UI/API against env.OAUTH_PROVIDER helpers. README documents listing active grants for a user and revoking/deleting a grant; deleteClient cascades and revokes all grants/tokens for that client. README does not show a first-class RFC7009 /revoke endpoint option, so the plan's POST /revoke endpoint should be treated as a Yutome app endpoint or verified against package types before assuming the library provides it automatically.
8. Schedule purgeExpiredData via a Worker cron. The library uses KV TTLs and purgeExpiredData as defense-in-depth for expired/orphaned grants/tokens; cron needs repeated batched calls because of subrequest limits.
9. Consent/authorize UI must keep CSRF/state protections. Cloudflare securing guide recommends one-time CSRF cookies, HTML sanitization for client metadata, strict CSP/frame-ancestors none, and short-lived KV state binding between consent and callback.
10. If supporting latest MCP Client ID Metadata Documents later, enable clientIdMetadataDocumentEnabled and add global_fetch_strictly_public for SSRF protection. This is not required by the existing plan's immediate DCR path but is a current-spec compatibility item.

Plan conflict/delta flags, without overriding docs/hosted-provider-broker-plan.md:
- Plan references MCP 2025-06-18 and lists dynamic client registration /register as a hosted endpoint. Latest MCP 2025-11-25 says DCR is optional/fallback and Client ID Metadata Documents are SHOULD. Recommendation: keep /register for compatibility now, but do not make it the only client registration strategy in design notes/tests.
- Plan says GET /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource or equivalent. title: Phase 5 hosted semantic/hybrid MCP query gating
description: Wire hosted MCP semantic and hybrid find modes through Voyage query embedding and SearchStore semantic/hybrid query paths with UsageGate preflight for voyage.embed_query and search_store query operations.
acceptance_criteria: Hosted find supports semantic and hybrid modes with denial-before-embedding behavior, Voyage query usage normalization, search-store usage ledger events, provider failure classification, and tests for success/denial/failure paths; lexical mode remains provider-free.
notes:
2026-05-26 research packet for hosted semantic/hybrid MCP query gating; source untouched. Decisions relied on: docs/hosted-provider-broker-plan.md is authoritative; UsageGate/ledger enforce before provider/service work and Polar only mirrors; lexical MCP remains service_internal search_store only and provider-free; semantic/hybrid must preflight search_store.semantic_query/hybrid_query and voyage.embed_query before vector recall/provider call. Existing reusable code: src/yutome/embeddings.py:147 _embed_voyage_query already calls voyageai.Client.embed([query], input_type="query", output_dimension=dimension) and delegates hosted usage to _execute_hosted_voyage_call at src/yutome/embeddings.py:178, which wraps execute_provider_call and normalize_voyage_embeddings_response; src/yutome/hosted/normalizers.py:75 records total_tokens, vectors, output_dimension, input_type; src/yutome/hosted/provider_wrappers.py:58 reserves before call, emits provider_attempt_started/succeeded/failed, and classifies exceptions; src/yutome/hosted/errors.py:19 maps 401/403 auth, 402/quota, 429 rate_limit, 5xx transient. Search paths already exist in src/yutome/hosted/search_store.py:42 protocol and PostgresVectorChordSearchStore.semantic_search/hybrid_search at lines 127/143; semantic_query_plan/hybrid_query_plan at lines 237/269 record query_vector_dimensions, candidate limits, backend metadata. Current MCP adapter at src/yutome/hosted/mcp_query.py:167 only reserves lexical_query and calls lexical_search; HostedFindRequest rejects mode semantic/hybrid at lines 249-260.

Implementation write set to avoid conflicts with resource/read workers: own src/yutome/hosted/mcp_query.py and tests/test_hosted_mcp_query.py for this slice. Do not touch hosted resource/read/list/show/q adapters, Cloudflare Worker, HTTP API, migrations, or SearchStore SQL unless a test exposes a tiny contract mismatch. If implementation needs a new Voyage usage context hook, add it inside mcp_query.py rather than changing shared provider/search-store abstractions. Use existing _embed_voyage_query from src/yutome/embeddings.py, estimate_voyage_embeddings and estimate_search_store_query from src/yutome/hosted/allocation_policy.py. Preferred flow: parse mode into lexical|semantic|hybrid; lexical preserves current path and never constructs Voyage client/context. For semantic/hybrid, reserve search_store first so search quota denial stops before Voyage; then reserve voyage.embed_query; only after both are allowed call _embed_voyage_query; then call search_store.semantic_search or hybrid_search; append search_store service_operation_succeeded after search completes. Risk: if search_store reservation succeeds but Voyage reservation denies/fails, current event model has no explicit reservation release/cancel event; record the Voyage denial/failure and do not emit search_store success. If that orphan-reservation concern blocks durable accounting, file a follow-up for reservation cancellation semantics rather than expanding this worker's scope.

Exact tests to add/update in tests/test_hosted_mcp_query.py: (1) semantic denial-before-embedding when search_store semantic_query balance/policy denies: fake Voyage client/context must not be constructed, store.calls empty, one denied search_store event. (2) semantic denial-before-embedding when voyage.embed_query denies after search preflight: no Voyage client construction and no search_store call; ledger has denied voyage event with mcp metadata. (3) provider failure classification for Voyage query embedding: fake voyage embed raises 429 and 503 variants; ledger order is provider_attempt_started then provider_attempt_failed with failure_kind rate_limit/transient and retryable true; adapter surfaces a HostedMcpError/clear failure without calling SearchStore. (4) usage event order for semantic success: voyage provider_attempt_started, voyage provider_attempt_succeeded with actual total_tokens/vectors/input_type=query/output_dimension, then search_store service_operation_succeeded semantic_query with query_vector_dimensions/result_count/backend/index_profile metadata. (5) hybrid success mirrors semantic but calls hybrid_search(query, query_vector) and records search_store.hybrid_query candidate_limit/result_limit/query_vector_dimensions. (6) lexical provider-free behavior: mode omitted or lexical keeps hosted_find_defaulted_to_lexical behavior, never touches Voyage, only emits search_store.lexical_query; regression for unsupported semantic/hybrid rejection should be replaced. (7) search-store events: actual_units must merge usage.units plus result_count len(rows) fallback for semantic/hybrid as lexical currently does; rows map match_type/scores through _contract_find_row. Existing coverage to preserve: tests/test_hosted_search_store.py already covers semantic/hybrid SQL and usage; tests/test_hosted_embedding_integration.py already covers _embed_voyage_query usage normalization and denial; tests/test_hosted_provider_wrappers.py covers provider failure events.

Credentials/env for real-world smoke, no secrets exposed: .env.example lines 3-4 and src/yutome/cli.py lines 149-150 reference VOYAGE_API_KEY for semantic/hybrid; Voyage SDK also auto-reads VOYAGE_API_KEY. .env.example lines 22-24 and src/yutome/env.py lines 49-51 reference GEMINI_API_KEY/GOOGLE_API_KEY but those are not needed for this MCP query slice. Webshare/Youtube envs exist in .env.example lines 12-20 and CLI template lines 157-165 but are unrelated. src/yutome/hosted/auth.py lines 12-27 forbids provider credential keys, including voyage_api_key, in hosted token/auth props. Real-world smoke option after implementation: if VOYAGE_API_KEY is present in the environment, run a manually marked/single test or script that uses _embed_voyage_query(query="crohn probiotics", model="voyage-4-lite", dimension=1024, hosted_context=recording context) and asserts vector length 1024 plus normalized total_tokens; do not print the key or raw env. Full DB smoke can stay fake-connection unless hosted Postgres creds are explicitly available.

Official Voyage docs lookup was needed/current. Official sources: https://docs.voyageai.com/docs/embeddings says voyageai.Client.embed(texts, model, input_type, output_dimension, output_dtype) and recommends input_type query/document for retrieval; voyage-4-lite supports 1024 default and flexible dimensions including 1024. https://docs.voyageai.com/reference/embeddings-api-1 documents POST https://api.voyageai.com/v1/embeddings, input_type query/document, output_dimension, output_dtype, and returned embeddings. https://docs.voyageai.com/docs/tokenization documents Client.count_tokens(texts, model) and that VOYAGE_API_KEY is auto-used by Client. https://docs.voyageai.com/docs/pricing states text embeddings are charged by token count in documents/queries and voyage-4-lite is $0.02/M tokens after free allowance. Option weighed: using local character/token_count estimate vs Voyage count_tokens for preflight. Use count_tokens when practical for precise hosted reservations; acceptable fallback for tests/offline is conservative ceil(len(query)/5) with method metadata, but do not call embed before UsageGate allows.

Risks/open points: current mcp_query UsageContextProvider only returns search_store context; implementation needs a narrow way to build ProviderCallContext for voyage.embed_query without leaking provider credentials into auth. Idempotency key must include mode, text, limit, project, grant/client/session, and operation (voyage.embed_query vs search_store.semantic_query/hybrid_query) to avoid lexical/semantic collisions. SearchStore uses pgvector/FTS fallback labels while VectorChord BM25 remains not_implemented; preserve metadata so billing/ops can distinguish fallback SQL. Leave bead open for implementation worker.
2026-05-26 implementation closeout: inspected current workspace after Curie commit 5a49e7f and preserved resource/read/show behavior in src/yutome/hosted/mcp_query.py and tests/test_hosted_mcp_query.py. Semantic/hybrid MCP find now routes through UsageGate search_store.semantic_query/hybrid_query first, builds a ProviderCallContext for voyage.embed_query only after search quota passes, calls the injectable query_embedder production default backed by _embed_voyage_query, and then calls SearchStore.semantic_search/hybrid_search. Lexical mode remains provider-free. Provider denial maps to usage_denied without embedding/search; provider HTTP failures map to structured provider_call_failed while preserving provider_attempt_started/failed ledger events. Tests added/verified cover search denial before embedding, Voyage denial before provider/search, provider failure classification, semantic/hybrid success event order and metadata, lexical provider-free behavior, and search-store actual usage/result_count metadata. Verification: uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_mcp_query.py tests/test_hosted_embedding_integration.py tests/test_hosted_provider_wrappers.py tests/test_hosted_usage.py -q => 47 passed. Risk noted: no reservation cancellation event exists when search preflight succeeds but Voyage reservation denies/fails; no search-store success is emitted in that path.

7. Production Worker config is still connector_only in wrangler.toml and the parent notes record production workers.dev health as connector_only. Hosted staging exists by Wrangler deployment metadata, but custom domain and production routing are not accepted.

Non-blocking/post-V1 follow-ups or lower priority once blockers clear:

- Treat lexical hosted find as a good intermediate slice: tests pass and usage/workspace isolation are real, but it is not enough for Phase 5 acceptance.
- Next best code slice after in-progress child work: account-backed OAuth/revocation path or resource/show parity, depending on whether live domain/API deployment is unblocked first.

Recommendation:

Overage credit math + Stripe metered Price are already built and tested (B2).

title: Hosted: surface the dual MCP + HTTP API offering (like scry.io's two-front-doors)
description: User concern + workflow finding: Yutome HAS both an MCP server and a bearer-token HTTP API (find/list/show/q) but the dual offering is stated ONLY in dev/architecture docs (query-api.md:1, remote-access.md:59) and a README packaging aside (README.md:17). The hosted product surfaces ONLY MCP: landing (home.tsx:21), dashboard 'Connect your assistant' (dashboard.home.tsx:230 shows only mcpUrl), connect-guides + mcp-menu are MCP-only, and hosted mode has NO end-user API-key issuance path (hosted.md:611 only has MCP-token/dashboard-session/CLI-token auth). scry.io markets both as two front doors over one bearer key. FIX: (1) dashboard 'Use from code' card issuing+showing a personal API token + a copyable curl against the hosted base; requires a hosted end-user API-key path (reuse CLI PKCE/token machinery /account/cli/authorize+token). (2) extend landing/README one-liner to 'query two ways: MCP URL or bearer HTTP API' (pull wording from query-api.md:1-7). (3) promote query-api.md/remote-access.md to a linked Developer API page + expose FastAPI /docs. Defer scry-style usage pricing; key-gated on existing plan/UsageGate (private corpus). product-design.md:9,54 already endorses this audience.
notes: AUDIT CONFIRMED the core gap: hosted has NO end-user/developer API-key issuance. The MCP query endpoints (/tools/call) take a shared infra token (YUTOME_HOSTED_API_TOKEN, for the Cloudflare edge only) + a workspace header the edge supplies; the only end-user credential is the CLI PKCE token (loopback-bound, scoped to source-import + jobs/library reads, CANNOT reach /tools/call). So a hosted developer has no supported way to call find/show over HTTP for their own workspace. FRONTEND DONE (fffcb5d): landing+README+query-api now frame the HTTP API honestly as available via self-host/CLI (bearer via 'serve remote prepare'), not a hosted key. REMAINING (src/): add hosted API-key issuance (create/list/revoke endpoints + table), bind keys to a workspace+scopes for the find/list/show/q HTTP endpoints, and a dashboard key-management UI.
close_reason: Personal API-key backend + HTTP API front door. api_keys table (schema+raw DDL, parity test updated), src/yutome/hosted/api_keys.py (256-bit keys, sha256 hash, workspace+scope bound). Account-auth'd create/list/revoke (raw key once; list hides hash; workspace-scoped revoke; active+non-revoked validation) + /account/keys/find|list|show|q bearer surface. Full suite 440 passed.

title: Evaluate hosted data-access architecture (Core-string-gen + raw psycopg vs psycopg-native pool/Jsonb vs SQLAlchemy Engine)
description: Decision blocker for further hosted SQL work. Codebase uses SQLAlchemy Core only as a SQL-string generator (compile_postgres_statement -> raw psycopg3.execute), which pays for SA dependency/compile cost but gets none of execution-time value (typing/pooling/result mapping). Forces _json_param/_json_value + ::jsonb cast rules (80 call sites/10 modules) + hand-rolled ThreadLocalConnection (unbounded, no reset). Full RFC with options A/B/C, decision matrix, implementation sketches, risks, unknowns I did not verify, what I ruled out, sources, and a calibrated recommendation in docs/architecture/hosted-data-access-rfc.md. Resolve this before further Core conversions or hosted-sql.md changes.
notes:
Workstream D spec ready (workflow wf_21b0b538-292): implement the RFC's 'A-minus' pool-only option — replace unbounded ThreadLocalConnection (runtime.py:769-798) with a bounded psycopg_pool.ConnectionPool (add psycopg-pool dep; NOT bundled with psycopg[binary]). Per-thread checkout proxy preserves the 'with conn.transaction()' multi-statement-on-one-connection invariant; FastAPI lifespan drains close(). Jsonb()/_json_param cleanups stay OUT of scope. Overlaps runtime.py with workstream C — add pool alongside, switch call sites.
Pool-only (A-minus) IMPLEMENTED in worktree-agent-a6abe44bc425b7299 (branch). NOTE: this worktree's codebase predated the spec — there was NO ThreadLocalConnection class; the connection facade was a direct connect_postgres() (autocommit=True, dict_row) cached on HostedCommandRunner.connection, and the only callers were runtime.py + http_api.py + cli.py (no cli/actions.py or api.py._connect). Adapted accordingly.

Changes: (1) pyproject.toml: added 'psycopg-pool>=3.2,<4' after psycopg[binary]; uv sync -> psycopg-pool 3.3.1. (2) runtime.py: removed connect_postgres; added HostedConnectionPoolSettings (frozen dc, from_env reads YUTOME_POSTGRES_POOL_MAX_SIZE=10/MIN_SIZE=1/MAX_IDLE_SECONDS=300/MAX_LIFETIME_SECONDS=3600, clamps min<=max); added build_connection_pool() (same 'Set YUTOME_POSTGRES_URL or DATABASE_URL' error; configure cb sets autocommit=True+dict_row; open=False then tolerant open(wait=False) for cold-start resilience; atexit.register(pool.close) so one-shot CLI exits clean); added PooledConnection proxy (threading.local, getconn-per-thread, putconn on release(), __getattr__ delegation) preserving the with-conn.transaction() multi-statement-on-one-physical-connection invariant; HostedCommandRunner gains pool_settings field, _pool field, pool property, close(); connect() builds pool lazily and returns a cached PooledConnection (injected connection still short-circuits). (3) http_api.py: build_postgres_app/build_app take pool=; FastAPI(lifespan=...) closes pool on shutdown (idempotent, no-op when pool None); per-request sync Depends release_pooled_connection() yields then calls proxy.release() in finally -> runs on the same threadpool worker thread as the sync handler (correct thread-affinity), attached to call_tool + read_resource.

Verified against disposable Postgres (docker postgres:16, no persistent DSN): autocommit write survives putconn reset-on-return (reset rollback is a no-op with autocommit=True + already-committed tx); transaction() commits atomically on one connection; rollback on exception; 6 concurrent threads on max_size=2 stay bounded with thread-affine release; FastAPI lifespan sets pool.closed=True on shutdown. 448 passed / 2 skipped (DB tests skip w/o DSN). Ruff: changed files add only the FastAPI Depends-in-defaults B008 idiom already used by the adjacent Depends(auth) lines.

Still OUT of scope (file as follow-ups): Jsonb()/_json_param + _rows_from_result unification (RFC Direction A steps 2-3). Issue left OPEN per request.
CORRECTION: the pool-only implementation attempt ran in an isolation worktree that branched from main (merge-base, 66 commits behind codex/yt-indexer-z6t), so it replaced a connect_postgres that does not exist on the working branch and never touched the real ThreadLocalConnection (runtime.py:769-798). Code discarded. Design is sound and transferable: psycopg_pool.ConnectionPool per HostedCommandRunner; per-thread checkout proxy (threading.local) preserving the connection facade + with conn.transaction() on one physical connection; release via a sync Depends generator (same threadpool thread); FastAPI lifespan drains pool.close(); atexit close for one-shot CLI; envs YUTOME_POSTGRES_POOL_MAX_SIZE(10)/MIN_SIZE(1)/MAX_IDLE_SECONDS(300)/MAX_LIFETIME_SECONDS(3600); open=False then open(wait=False) for boot resilience; autocommit + reset-on-return verified no-op. RE-IMPLEMENT against codex/ runtime.py (replace ThreadLocalConnection) AFTER the Stripe work (yt-indexer-zcw) lands, since both edit runtime.py. Do NOT use worktree isolation in this repo.
close_reason: Direction A: psycopg_pool.ConnectionPool behind the .execute()/.transaction() facade, per-request connection lease (leak-free: released on all paths + pool drain on shutdown), stripe_webhook via run_in_threadpool, JSONB->Jsonb migration. Live-Postgres tests pass; full suite 429 passed. Committed.

title: Hosted ops: codify Railway API, worker, cron, and maintenance services
description: Why this exists: hosted runtime code exists for the API, worker, source-refresh tick, maintenance tick, and billing-export worker, but the repository does not codify the Railway service layout or start commands. What needs to be done: add or document deploy configuration for Railway services api, worker, cron-source-refresh, cron-maintenance, and billing-export, including health checks, start commands, variables, cron cadence, restart policy, and live smoke steps.
acceptance_criteria: Repository or docs define the hosted Railway services and their commands; source-refresh and maintenance ticks are scheduled as global idempotent cron ticks; worker and billing export services have clear start commands and required variables; live deployment smoke verifies API readiness, worker job claim, source refresh enqueue, maintenance lease cleanup, and billing export no-op/configured behavior.
notes:
Design constraint: hosted operations are still in development mode. Railway service config/start commands may break or replace previous manual deployment assumptions; codify the clean target service layout rather than preserving ad hoc deployment compatibility.
Workstream C spec ready (workflow wf_21b0b538-292): add HostedScheduler + 'hosted run scheduler --loop/--once' (new job value, not a new top-level cmd) ticking worker_once/source_refresh_tick/maintenance_tick/billing_export_once at per-activity cadences, plus balance_rollover_once + open_next_workspace_balance_period_sql (Core) re-seeding remaining_units from EntitlementPolicy.included_units (carry-nothing default). All claims already use FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED so multi-replica is safe. Note: billing_export tick couples to Stripe rewrite (yt-indexer-zcw).
LAUNCH GATE surfaced by audit: the Stripe webhook (billing.stripe_webhook_processing_statements) only upserts the customer + flips workspaces.subscription_status — it does NOT provision an EntitlementPolicy or seed a WorkspaceBalance. Entitlement is seeded once at workspace bootstrap (the Personal/trial allowance) and there is NO monthly rollover yet (this issue). Consequence: a SUBSCRIBED workspace keeps period-1's allowance but it never refreshes next month -> paid users eventually hard-capped at the gate. Before paid launch: (1) confirm a subscribed workspace actually has a usable EntitlementPolicy+balance after checkout, and (2) ship the monthly balance rollover so the included allowance refreshes per period.
DONE+committed 1187e65: (a) webhook now provisions starter EntitlementPolicy+WorkspaceBalance on active/trialing subscription (idempotent, preserves mid-period remaining); (b) balance_rollover_tick_sql + balance_rollover_once + 'hosted run balance-rollover' job (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, carry-nothing, re-seeds from active policy included_units, month-snap; live-Postgres idempotency test). LAUNCH-CRITICAL OPS REMAINING: wire Railway cron to run the single-shot workers on a schedule — worker_once (jobs, frequent), source_refresh_tick, maintenance_tick, balance_rollover (hourly: 'hosted run balance-rollover --once'), billing_export_once (dormant while overage off). Without the rollover cron, subscribers cap after period 1. MINOR FOLLOW-UP: webhook-provision upsert preserves an EXPIRED period (only resets remaining/policy), so a subscribe landing in the <=1h window after period-expiry before the next rollover tick is briefly un-entitled; consider conditionally snapping period to current month on subscribe-activate.
RAILWAY CRON PLAN (researched from Railway docs + verified against cli/hosted.py). Railway cron = per-service Cron Schedule (dashboard or deploy.cronSchedule in railway.json/toml); process MUST exit; 5-field UTC crontab, 5-min minimum, overlap=skip. Verified CLI args: worker/source-refresh/maintenance/balance-rollover/stripe-meter-export; --once=single pass, loop=omit --once (NO --loop flag); maintenance + balance-rollover take no --lease-owner. SHAPE: 6 services sharing image+Postgres: web (existing), worker (long-running, no cron, --poll-interval 5, restart On-Failure), + 4 cron services (restart NEVER): source-refresh '*/5 * * * *', maintenance '*/15 * * * *', balance-rollover '0 * * * *' (hourly), stripe-meter-export '*/15' kept DISABLED until overage launches. web must NOT get a cron schedule. SKIP LOCKED makes overlap safe. Docs: docs.railway.com/cron-jobs, /config-as-code/reference, /deployments/restart-policy. Full synthesized plan in workflow w9lawnoet output.
Decision record + runbook persisted at docs/architecture/railway-ops.md (committed) + bd memory 'railway-ops-scheduling'. title: Hosted: spike SQLFluff pglast Atlas guardrails
description: Evaluate SQLFluff, pglast/libpg_query, and Atlas as guardrails. Lint-only first; no formatting/autofix; Atlas optional until it proves useful without forcing churn.
acceptance_criteria: Spike records what each tool catches/cannot catch; any adopted check is narrow, non-mutating, and has low false-positive noise; extension-heavy SQL remains validated by live Postgres.
notes: Research/verification agent Singer confirmed current local tooling: Docker/OrbStack and psql are available; atlas, sqlfluff, and pglast are not installed yet. Recommendation: evaluate these as narrow, non-mutating checks only. pglast/libpg_query can help parse simple extracted SQL but will not validate extension semantics or parameter typing. SQLFluff may help style/splitting but should start lint-only with no autofix. Atlas may help drift visibility after Alembic metadata exists, but should not force an ORM-style schema workflow. Live Postgres remains authoritative for placeholder casts, arrays, ON CONFLICT, extension SQL, and VectorChord behavior.
close_reason: Adopted the useful low-noise guardrail now: Ruff E9/F for fast Python correctness feedback. Kept SQLFluff/pglast/Atlas out of the implementation because live Postgres, Core compilation, Alembic metadata, and VectorChord smoke cover the current error classes without style churn.

Decisions relied on: UsageGate/Yutome ledger remains authoritative for pre-call authorization; Polar is a billing/customer-state mirror only; durable exports are locally keyed by billing export idempotency key plus provider/source_event_dedupe_key so replay can be idempotent before and after Polar calls; webhook snapshots store raw payload and customer/subscription state for later entitlement updates without authorizing current runtime work.

title: Fix hosted vector dimension/index profile correctness
description: Address Claude review P2: make hosted Postgres search-store vector dimensions and index profile behavior explicit. notes: Decisions: current hosted schema uses a fixed vector(1024) embedding column and now treats 1024 as the only supported hosted embedding profile until a schema/index migration adds separate support for Voyage flexible dimensions. Default profile is voyage-4-lite/1024d, matching Voyage docs default and the VectorChord/pgvector fixed-dimension index shape. Unsupported dimensions fail early via search-store profile/query-vector validation and via schema CHECK constraints. FTS lexical and hybrid fallback usage now reports fallback backends with storage_backend metadata rather than overclaiming VectorChord BM25 execution. Options weighed: unconstrained vector column with per-profile expression/partial indexes versus fixed vector(1024). Chose fixed 1024 because current schema/index are fixed-dimension and the slice scope excludes a multi-profile storage migration. Files touched: src/yutome/hosted/migrations.py, src/yutome/hosted/search_store.py, tests/test_hosted_postgres.py, tests/test_hosted_search_store.py. Residual risks: existing hosted indexing helper still has an 8d mock default outside this worker write scope, so it will now fail clearly against this store contract until that helper is aligned or test-only profiles are isolated.

title: Hosted: wire YouTube OAuth connect flow into web dashboard
description: Why this exists: the hosted web dashboard only accepts public YouTube source URLs (dashboard.home.tsx 'Add a source'); there is no way for a hosted user to connect their YouTube account and import their subscriptions. The control-plane already models a YouTube grant (control_plane.YouTubeGrant, youtube_grants table, Source.requires_youtube_grant), but no web route initiates the OAuth consent, persists the grant, or exposes a 'Connect YouTube' affordance, so a source with requires_youtube_grant is never discoverable. The local CLI imports subscriptions via browser cookies (youtube_import.fetch_user_subscription_channels_from_browser); that path is local-only by construction (reads the on-disk browser cookie store) and cannot run hosted, which is exactly why hosted needs the OAuth path. What needs to be done: add the web routes + UI to start YouTube read-only OAuth consent, handle the callback, create a YouTube grant scoped to source discovery, and let the user create subscription sources against it.
acceptance_criteria: Hosted user can start YouTube OAuth consent from the dashboard and return with a stored, active YouTube grant; user can create a subscriptions source that becomes discoverable only while the grant is active; revoked/expired grants surface a reconnect affordance and stop discovery; public source add continues to work without any YouTube grant.
notes: Depends on the grant storage/refresh/revocation contract in yt-indexer-59p; this issue is the user-facing wiring on top of it. Use the canonical glossary term 'YouTube grant' (read-only subscription discovery, separated from Yutome account/session auth and from provider allocations). Scope: read-only YouTube scope only; the grant must not authorize provider spend. UI lives on the dashboard (the old dashboard.connect.tsx was removed; connect now lives in the home hero). Public URL/handle source add must keep working with no YouTube grant.
close_reason: Implemented hosted dashboard YouTube OAuth connect/callback, subscription listing, and selected-channel import. Selected subscriptions are imported as public channel sources for V1; yt-indexer-rmd tracks first-class subscription collection executor work.

title: Define per-call hosted Gemini idempotency for fan-out
description: Hosted Gemini transcription and cleanup can fan out into multiple generate_content calls. The Phase 1 Gemini slice accepts one ProviderCallContext and applies it to each request, preserving opt-in behavior and denial enforcement. A hosted control-plane follow-up should decide whether callers must synthesize per-window/per-batch idempotency keys, estimates, and metadata for reconciliation.
notes: Implemented per-call hosted Gemini context derivation for fan-out. Decisions relied on: UsageGate/Yutome ledger remain the pre-call authorization source; provider wrappers stay opt-in by only deriving contexts when ProviderCallContext is supplied; retries must get deterministic keys that are stable across job reruns but distinct for additional paid generate_content attempts. Options weighed: reusing the parent operation key was rejected because multi-window/multi-batch calls collide in reservations/events; moving logic into hosted wrappers was avoided to keep wrappers generic and preserve explicit caller state/effects; arbitrary token-estimate splitting was avoided because this slice lacks per-batch count_tokens data. Transcription windows now suffix parent idempotency with window identity and attach window metadata, deriving media_seconds only when exact bounds are known. Cleanup batches now suffix by batch/sequence window, and validation retries suffix by attempt. Files touched: src/yutome/gemini.py, src/yutome/quality_llm.py, tests/test_hosted_gemini_integration.py. Verification: uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_gemini_integration.py tests/test_hosted_provider_wrappers.py tests/test_hosted_usage.py -q passed (26 passed). Live Gemini smoke skipped because GEMINI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI, and GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT were not present; no low-cost real-call smoke was available without credentials. Ruff file-scope check attempted but skipped because ruff is not installed in the environment. Residual risks: cleanup token estimates are still inherited from caller context rather than recomputed per batch; full no-duplicate-provider-result replay after a successful provider response remains a later durable operation-state concern outside these files.
close_reason: Completed: implemented stable per-window, per-batch, and per-cleanup-attempt hosted Gemini ProviderCallContext derivation with focused tests; required hosted pytest suite passes.
